title: NVIDIA confirms GeForce NOW data breach affecting Armenian users
description: NVIDIA has confirmed in a statement for BleepingComputer that GeForce NOW user information has been exposed in a data breach. [...]
author: Bill Toulas

title: Why More Analysts Won’t Solve Your SOC’s Alert Problem
description: Attackers move faster than overwhelmed SOC teams can realistically investigate alerts. Prophet Security breaks down how AI can help analysts investigate alerts faster and focus on real threats. [...]
author: Sponsored by Prophet Security

title: Trellix source code breach claimed by RansomHouse hackers
description: The attack on the Trellix source code repository disclosed last week has been claimed by the RansomHouse threat group, which leaked a small set of images as proof of the intrusion. [...]
author: Bill Toulas

title: CISA gives feds four days to patch Ivanti flaw exploited as zero-day
description: CISA has given U.S. federal agencies four days to secure their networks against a high-severity vulnerability in Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]
author: Sergiu Gatlan

title: Zara data breach exposed personal information of 197,000 people
description: Hackers who gained access to the databases of Spanish fast-fashion retailer Zara stole data belonging to more than 197,000 customers, according to data breach notification service Have I Been Pwned. [...]
author: Sergiu Gatlan

title: Former govt contractor convicted for wiping dozens of federal databases
description: A 34-year-old Virginia man was found guilty of conspiring to destroy dozens of government databases after getting fired from his job as a federal contractor. [...]
author: Sergiu Gatlan

title: New Linux 'Dirty Frag' zero-day gives root on all major distros
description: A new Linux zero-day exploit, named Dirty Frag, allows local attackers to gain root privileges on most major Linux distributions with a single command. [...]
author: Sergiu Gatlan
